title: Ref-Qualified Value Reporter
prompt_md:
## Goal
Complete class `Holder` with two ref-qualified overloads of `report()` that print different prefixes depending on whether the object is an lvalue or rvalue.

## Input
Line 1: integer V.

## Output
Two lines:
- Line 1: `Named: <V>` (from calling `report()` on a named variable — lvalue overload)
- Line 2: `Temp: <V>` (from calling `report()` on a temporary — rvalue overload)

## Requirements
1. `Holder` stores an integer value `m_val` as a private member.
2. `void report() &` prints `Named: <m_val>` followed by a newline.
3. `void report() &&` prints `Temp: <m_val>` followed by a newline.
4. `main()` reads V, creates a named `Holder h{V}`, calls `h.report()`, then calls `Holder{V}.report()`.
5. Edge case: V = 0 should still print both lines with value 0.

title: Safe Getter Bug Fix
prompt_md:
## Goal
The program below uses ref-qualified getters to safely return a value from both lvalue and rvalue `Packet` objects. It compiles and runs but prints the wrong value on one of the lines. Find and fix the one logic bug.

## Input
Line 1: integer N.

## Output
Two lines:
- Line 1: `Lvalue: <N>`
- Line 2: `Rvalue: <N>`

## Requirements
1. Both lines should print the same value N.
2. The bug is a logic error — it produces the wrong number on one of the lines.
3. Hidden tests include N = 0 and negative N.
